column7: "This is the neighborhood pool for Spring Ridge
column8: the neighborhood I grew up in. There was a summer league swim team that I swam on every summer
column9: and that was how I found my love for swimming! The facility has 4 pools

column6: "The C. Burr Artz Public Library is located along the Carroll Creek in downtown Frederick! The library has information in all medias from books to videos
column7: and the architecture of the building is nothing like you've seen in other libraries!" https://assets.simpleviewinc.com/simpleview/image/fetch/q_75/https://assets.simpleviewinc.com/simpleview/image/upload/crm/frederickcountymd/library0-747d71365056a36_747d7240-5056-a36a-07130a27b3b37b85.jpg

column6: "The Delaplaine is the art hub of Frederick
column10: and family programs. They feature work from local and regional artists
column11: but also include art from the schools in the area as well! When I was in elementary school
column12: one of my art projects was featured in their gallery!"https://assets.simpleviewcms.com/simpleview/image/fetch/q_75/https://res.cloudinary.com/simpleview/image/upload/crm/frederickcountymd/DSC09951_B2FBCF10-CCA9-406A-8504808FBAD05E08_b215193a-1963-4197-aba769e3de95cfd6.jpg

column6: "Baker Park is a 58 acre park in the heart of downtown and includes jogging and bike paths
column9: and even a venue for concerts
column11: and festivals! My favorite memory as a child was feeding the duck in the lake every Sunday!" https://images.fineartamerica.com/images/artworkimages/mediumlarge/3/baker-park-carrilon-frederick-md-david-smith.jpg

column5: "Carroll Creek Park runs through downtown Frederick and along the river there are many local shops and restaurants. It's a great place for jogging
column7: and shopping all while enjoying the beautiful view!" https://assets.simpleviewinc.com/sv-frederick-county/image/upload/c_fill

column6: "This beautiful park is perfect for hikes and backpacking trips
column7: with an entrance to the Appalachian Trail! Hiking is one of my favorite things to do with my sister and friends
column8: and Gathland State Park was a popular place for us to go."https://www.housewivesoffrederickcounty.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/National-War-Correspondents-Memorial-Arch2.jpg

column6: "Not only is the Civil War Museum a great stop for any history lovers
column7: it's also significant to capturing the pivotal role Maryland's border towns in shaping the American Civil War. This museum introduced me to more of Maryland's history beyond what my history classes taught me!"https://www.marylandhauntedhouses.com/real-haunt-photo/national-museum-civil-war-medicine_3475.jpg

column1: Pistarro's is an Italian restaurant local to Frederick, serving pizza, pasta, and salumi. The ambient lighting and live music are the perfect atmosphere for a night out! https://imagescdn.homes.com/i2/8K5pbnZB0QBG4NRpE5ctEI0vGOWcPCI19LGUhjM7B20/117/carrollton-frederick-md.jpg?p=1

column7: named after the Frederick native and author of The Star Spangled Banner
column8: Francis Scott Key. This small mall packs a brunch with an array of stores without being overcrowded. If you make a wish while throwing a penny into the fountain
column9: it is said that your wish will come true!" https://live.staticflickr.com/65535/53912043854_1935b00c11_h.jpg

column6: "Frederick Fudge & Ice Cream Co is your one stop shot for any sweet cravings. Don't like ice cream? No worries! They also sell fudge and chocolate
column7: so there's something for everyone! Getting ice cream there every Friday is my favorite tradition with my sister! "https://media-cdn.tripadvisor.com/media/photo-s/0b/ec/7b/22/frederick-fudge-ice-cream.jpg
